Center: US National Nuclear Data Center, Brookhaven, USA
ref: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.28, p.2212 (1983)
title: Optical model analysis of scattering of 7- to 15-MeV neutrons from 1-p shell nuclei

x4freetext: Optical model analysis of scattering of 7- to 15-MeV
neutrons from 1-p shell nuclei

x4codes.hlp: North Carolina State University, Raleigh, NC, United States of America
Triangle Universities Nuclear Lab., Durham, NC, United States of America

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Physical Review, Part C, Nuclear Physics, Vol.28, p.2212 (1983)

x4codes.hlp: Triangle Universities Nuclear Lab., Durham, NC, United States of America

x4freetext: Experiments carried out at the TUNL
FN tandem Van de Graaff neutron time of flight
facility
Scattered neutrons detected by two NE218
liquid scintillation detectors. 8.9 cm x 5.08 cm
detector was positioned at a flight path of 4m,
12.71 cm x 5.08 cm detector at a flight path of
6 m. Each detector can be placed at angles of
0 deg to 160 deg. Detector bias was set to 1.9 MeV
neutron cut-off. Pulse shape discrimination was used
and detectors were shielded by a tungsten shadow
bar from direct neutron flux.
Neutrons produced by deuteron bombardment of
a 3 cm long deuterium gas cell filled to a pressure
of 2 atm. Beam current was typically 3 muA, pulsed
at 2 MHz repetition rate and bunched to a width
of about 2 ns
Typically 2.54 cm high and 1.27 to 1.91 cm in
diameter enclosed in aluminium cans.
Data corrected for
-effects of extended neutron source
-finite sample size
-finite detector size
Uncertainty of total eleastic cross section
estimated to be about 5% at higher energies.
Compiled by S.H.
SD: Subents 21-26 deleted (duplication with
Subents 27-32)
